017package org.apache.juneau.config;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.*;
020
021/**
022 * Auto-registers a classpath-default {@code juneau.cfg} (and the other names returned by
023 * {@link Config#getCandidateSystemDefaultConfigNames()}) as a {@link PropertySource} for the
024 * process-wide {@link Settings} singleton.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * Discovered by {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.Settings.Builder#useServiceLoader() ServiceLoader-based wiring} through a
028 * {@code META-INF/services/org.apache.juneau.commons.settings.PropertySourceProvider} entry
029 * shipped in this module.
030 *
031 * <p>
032 * Lookup semantics: the registered source defers calling {@link Config#getSystemDefault()} to the
033 * first {@code get(name)} invocation. This is deliberate — calling {@code getSystemDefault()} from
034 * inside {@link #create()} would create a static-initialization cycle because
035 * {@code Settings.<clinit>} fires the ServiceLoader at class-load time, and {@code Config}'s
036 * static initializers transitively reference {@link Settings} (through {@code Cache} / {@code Utils.env(...)})
037 * which would re-enter the {@code Settings.<clinit>} block.
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * Lazy resolution makes the cycle a non-issue: by the time {@code @Value("${...}")} fires its first
041 * lookup, all participating classes are fully initialized.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>Silence on missing config:</h5>
044 * <p>
045 * If no system-default {@link Config} is discoverable (no {@code juneau.cfg} / {@code default.cfg}
046 * / {@code application.cfg} / etc. on the classpath or working directory), {@code get(name)}
047 * returns {@link PropertyLookupResult#missing()} for every key.  No logging, no exception — many
048 * deployments have no classpath config and shouldn't see noise.
049 *
050 * <h5 class='section'>Ordering:</h5>
051 * <p>
052 * {@link #order()} returns {@code 100}, which is "low" in the {@link PropertySourceProvider}
053 * scheme — provider-discovered classpath defaults register near the bottom of the source list so
054 * that programmatic {@code Settings.get().addSource(...)} calls (e.g. per-microservice config or
055 * the Spring {@code Environment} bridge) shadow the classpath default.  {@link Settings} walks
056 * sources in reverse insertion order, so the most-recently-added source wins.
057 */
058public class ConfigPropertySourceProvider implements PropertySourceProvider {
059
060   @Override
061   public PropertySource create() {
062      // Return a lazy wrapper.  Resolving Config.getSystemDefault() eagerly here would trigger
063      // Config.<clinit>, which can transitively re-enter Settings.<clinit> through Cache /
064      // Utils.env(...) — the SPI registration is invoked from inside the Settings static init
065      // block, so any cycle here surfaces as ExceptionInInitializerError.
066      return new LazyConfigPropertySource();
067   }
068
069   @Override
070   public int order() {
071      // Low ordering: classpath defaults register first, so any programmatic
072      // addSource(...) added afterwards shadows the classpath defaults at lookup time
073      // (Settings walks sources in reverse insertion order).
074      return 100;
075   }
076
077   /**
078    * Defers {@link Config#getSystemDefault()} resolution to the first {@link #get(String)} call to
079    * avoid the static-initialization cycle described in the enclosing class' Javadoc.
080    *
081    * <p>
082    * Additionally protects against three runtime cycles at first-use time:
083    * <ol>
084    *    <li><b>Bootstrap-static-init cycle (poisoning).</b> If this source is called from inside
085    *       <i>any</i> JVM static-initializer chain that transitively loads {@code Config}, the
086    *       downstream {@code Config.findSystemDefault()} → {@code FileStore.<clinit>} →
087    *       {@code Context.Builder.build()} chain can fail with {@link NullPointerException}
088    *       because {@code Context.<clinit>} hasn't finished yet. That failure <i>poisons</i>
089    *       {@code FileStore} (every subsequent reference raises {@link NoClassDefFoundError}).
090    *       To prevent the poisoning, the source detects when it's called from a static-initializer
091    *       frame by walking the current stack for a {@code "<clinit>"} method name and short-circuits
092    *       to {@link PropertyLookupResult#missing()} <i>without</i> memoizing — so the next call,
093    *       from regular code, can resolve normally.
094    *    <li><b>Reentrant resolution cycle.</b> {@link Config#findSystemDefault()} looks up the
095    *       {@code "juneau.configFile"} system property via {@link Settings#get(String)}, which
096    *       walks every registered source — including this one. Without a guard, that re-enters
097    *       {@code Config.getSystemDefault()} on the same thread, which re-enters
098    *       {@code findSystemDefault()}, which re-enters {@code Settings.get("juneau.configFile")},
099    *       ad infinitum (a {@link StackOverflowError}). A {@link ThreadLocal} sentinel short-circuits
100    *       any re-entrant lookup on the same thread to {@code missing()}.
101    *    <li><b>Defensive throwable catch.</b> Any unexpected throwable (e.g. a different class
102    *       loader's {@code Config} init failure that the {@code <clinit>}-sniff missed) is
103    *       swallowed and treated as "not yet available" — {@code get(name)} returns
104    *       {@link PropertyLookupResult#missing()} without memoizing the failure.
105    * </ol>
106    */
107   @SuppressWarnings({
108      "java:S1181" // Intentional broad Throwable catch — see Javadoc.
109   })
110   static final class LazyConfigPropertySource implements PropertySource {
111
112      // Thread-local re-entrance guard. Static so it covers both the lazy-init path and any
113      // concurrent lookup that may also reach a Config.getSystemDefault() call (defensively).
114      private static final ThreadLocal<Boolean> RESOLVING = ThreadLocal.withInitial(() -> Boolean.FALSE);
115
116      // Guarded by the synchronized block in get(...); volatile reads cover the fast-path check.
117      private volatile boolean resolved;
118      @SuppressWarnings({
119         "java:S3077" // Publish-once reference: delegate is fully constructed before its single assignment inside the synchronized block, so volatile visibility is sufficient.
120      })
121      private volatile ConfigPropertySource delegate;
122
123      @Override
124      @SuppressWarnings({
125         "java:S3776" // Cognitive complexity acceptable for lazy config property source resolution
126      })
127      public PropertyLookupResult get(String name) {
128
129         // Re-entrant guard: Config.findSystemDefault() walks Settings.get("juneau.configFile"),
130         // which round-trips through every source including this one. Short-circuit on
131         // re-entry so the outer Config.getSystemDefault() can make progress.
132         if (Boolean.TRUE.equals(RESOLVING.get()))
133            return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
134
135         // Bootstrap guard: if we're being called from inside a <clinit> frame (e.g. another
136         // class's static-initializer chain transitively invokes Settings.get(...) → this
137         // source), don't attempt to resolve Config — doing so would force Config / FileStore /
138         // Context class loading from inside an already-in-progress class init, which poisons
139         // FileStore.<clinit> with a NoClassDefFoundError that survives the rest of the JVM
140         // lifetime. Short-circuit without memoizing; the next non-<clinit> call resolves.
141         if (! resolved && isInClinit())
142            return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
143
144         if (! resolved) {
145            synchronized (this) {
146               if (! resolved) {
147                  // Re-check the <clinit> guard inside the lock — another thread may have
148                  // flipped resolved between the first check and the lock acquire.
149                  if (isInClinit())
150                     return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
151
152                  Config cfg;
153                  RESOLVING.set(Boolean.TRUE);
154                  try {
155                     cfg = Config.getSystemDefault();
156                  } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") Throwable t) {
157                     // Defense-in-depth: a Config-side init failure the <clinit> sniff
158                     // missed. Return missing() without memoizing so the next lookup can
159                     // retry once Config init has completed.
160                     return PropertyLookupResult.missing();
161                  } finally {
162                     RESOLVING.remove();
163                  }
164                  // Silent when no juneau.cfg / default.cfg / etc. is discoverable — the
165                  // SPI consumer sees missing() for every key but no log noise.
166                  delegate = (cfg == null) ? null : new ConfigPropertySource(cfg);
167                  resolved = true;
168               }
169            }
170         }
171         return delegate == null ? PropertyLookupResult.missing() : delegate.get(name);
172      }
173
174      /**
175       * Returns <jk>true</jk> if any frame in the current thread's call stack is a Java
176       * static-initializer ({@code "<clinit>"}). Used to detect — and avoid — the bootstrap-time
177       * cycle that would otherwise poison {@code FileStore.<clinit>}.
178       *
179       * <p>
180       * Cost: a single {@link Thread#getStackTrace()} call on the first lookup per source-thread
181       * pair. {@code resolved} is volatile so subsequent calls skip the stack walk entirely.
182       *
183       * @return <jk>true</jk> if the current call is being made from inside a {@code <clinit>}
184       *    frame anywhere in the stack.
185       */
186      private static boolean isInClinit() {
187         var trace = Thread.currentThread().getStackTrace();
188         for (var frame : trace) {
189            if ("<clinit>".equals(frame.getMethodName()))
190               return true;
191         }
192         return false;
193      }
194   }
195}
